denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION
 Date of Notice: 02/16/2026
UnitedHealthcare Choice Plus (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W103040896          Group #: GRP-1384
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-156629
Claim #: CLM-2026-208353
Date of Service (requested): 01/27/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Central States Medical Associates
 Dear Member,
 This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 01/29/2026 for Myomectomy for fertility preservation related to Symptomatic uterine fibroids (D25.9). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request. CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Myomectomy for fertility preservation
Diagnosis (as submitted): Symptomatic uterine fibroids (D25.9)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service. Clinical policy applied: CDG-UM124 under UnitedHealthcare Coverage Determination Guideline (CDG) and applicable Medical Policy Bulletin
 EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CDG-UM124 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Prior authorization for Myomectomy for fertility preservation is denied because a required peer-to-peer review was not completed in the allowed window. Clinical information may be discussed during appeal submission. Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the utilization-management requirements associated with missing peer to peer. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval. Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards. If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials. YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal. APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W103040896) and reference PA-2026-156629. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination. Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above. Sincerely,
 UnitedHealthcare Utilization Management
Clinical Operations
clinical_context: The 27-year-old member has Symptomatic uterine fibroids (D25.9). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Myomectomy for fertility preservation recommended as the next appropriate step. Heavy bleeding Hgb 9.8; failed hormonal management; desires pregnancy. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that missing peer to peer bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.
